test_counts.note: This is a test-only integration story adding a new Playwright e2e spec (11 tests), not a Vitest unit-test story; the unit-test count is unchanged (11 e2e tests were added and verified separately via `make test-e2e-groups`-style filtered run, all 11 passing).

e2e_verification.note: Full-suite make test-e2e-all is the epic's Step 5.8 gate, run separately by the lead — not run here per story instructions.

findings.title: S3's returning-user branch change breaks 6 pre-existing prior-epic e2e specs
findings.detail: groups.tsx:950 now gates the full-screen JoinRequestCard behind `isFreshIdentity === true`. Every e2e-booted identity in this suite (via the shared addInitScript-seeds-localStorage-before-every-navigation bootUser pattern) reads as non-fresh at mount (deriveIsFreshIdentity finds a non-null stored identity), so these boot identities now land on the NEW list+banner returning-user experience instead of the full-screen card. Ground-truthed by running the unmodified prior-epic spec groups-invite-link.spec.ts: 2 of 11 tests fail with `getByTestId('join-request-card')` timeouts. The SAME `join-request-card` assertion pattern (a named boot-identity user expecting the full-screen card on a raw invite-link visit) also appears in groups-join-request-live.spec.ts, groups-approved-requester-label.spec.ts, groups-welcome.spec.ts, groups-join-request-profile.spec.ts, and groups-member-admitted-announcement.spec.ts (found via grep, not individually re-run). This is the epic's INTENDED behavior change surfacing as staleness in prior-epic e2e specs, not a bug in the new code — but it will make `make test-e2e-all` (the epic's Step 5.8 gate) fail on unrelated-looking prior-epic tests if not addressed before that gate runs. Not fixed here: out of this story's scope (no production-code changes; this story's scope names only the new spec file). Escalating to the architect/lead running the epic to decide: update the affected prior-epic specs to assert the new returning-user landing, or otherwise reconcile before the full-suite gate.
findings.evidence: app/tests/e2e/groups-invite-link.spec.ts run via `E2E_GROUPS=1 node scripts/run-e2e.mjs tests/e2e/groups-invite-link.spec.ts` — 2 failed / 6 did not run / 3 passed. Both failures at `getByTestId('join-request-card')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 30_000 })` (lines 116 and 269).
findings.recommendation: Update the 6 affected groups-*.spec.ts files' 'named returning user opens raw invite link' assertions to expect the new list+InviteAwaitingBanner landing (this epic's actual, intended behavior) instead of the full-screen JoinRequestCard, before running make test-e2e-all as the epic gate. Alternatively, if any of those specs specifically need to exercise the FIRST-VISIT (fresh-identity) path, they should boot without pre-seeding an identity via addInitScript so isFreshIdentity is genuinely true at mount.

retrospective.harder_than_needed: Determining the 'returning user' signal required tracing deriveIsFreshIdentity -> NostrIdentityContext's sole call site -> the existing bootUser e2e helper's addInitScript timing, rather than a documented answer; this also surfaced that the standard bootUser pattern ALWAYS yields isFreshIdentity=false (identity is seeded before every navigation, including reload()), which is not stated anywhere and only became clear by reading NostrIdentityProvider's init effect line-by-line. That same tracing exposed that 6 pre-existing prior-epic e2e specs now fail against the current (uncommitted) working tree for exactly this reason -- verified by actually running one of them before writing my own spec, rather than assuming the existing suite was green.
retrospective.surprised_by: The AC-OBS-2 adversarial construction (tight 250ms polling across the auto-accept transition, targeting the real gap where welcomeSubscription.ts's `deleteOutboundJoinRequest` fires synchronously but MarmotContext's onGroupJoined callback triggers `reloadGroups()` fire-and-forget, unawaited) did not reproduce a both-visible or neither-visible frame in 11/11 runs -- the theoretical race exists in the code's control flow but is apparently too fast (or React's batching absorbs it) for browser-level Playwright polling to observe. Recorded as a non-finding per the construction-must-reproduce-before-reporting rule, and surfaced as a post-impl verification question (VQ-S5-015) rather than a blocking finding.
